resposta:
solution: ```
class Solution:
    def maximumLengthSubstring(self, s: str) -> int:
        from collections import defaultdict
        left = 0
        max_len = 0
        freq = defaultdict(int)
        for right, ch in enumerate(s):
            freq[ch] += 1
            while freq[ch] > 2:
                freq[s[left]] -= 1
                left += 1
            max_len = max(max_len, right - left + 1)
        return max_len
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Sliding-window technique keeps a frequency count of characters in the current window. When any character exceeds two occurrences, the left pointer is advanced until the constraint is restored. This yields optimal O(n) time and O(1) space (26 letters max).
